page: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Edsv7 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for accelerated matrix operations. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a 945 GB temporary disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results place the E16ds_v7 in the top tier for single-threaded gzip compression at 36.52 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 15.37 GB/sec. It also achieves top-tier performance in LLM text generation using a small 135M model, reaching 343.48 tokens/sec. Other metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance at 18622.51 and PassMark database operations at 8890.67, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 136.15 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 56.03 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16ds_v7 offers a high density of memory and local storage relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads requiring substantial RAM without the expense of scaling up to higher vCPU tiers. The combination of 128 GB of RAM, a 945 GB local disk, and dedicated Intel Xeon processing makes this server well-suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and localized data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, powered by 16 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, alongside a 945 GB local storage disk. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, the server supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.52 GB/sec and small-model LLM text generation at 343.48 tokens/sec, while maintaining average performance in multi-core processing and database operations. This hardware profile makes the E16ds_v7 highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database management, caching layers, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: An Intel-powered, memory-optimized virtual server featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and fast local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory density
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation performance
Optimized for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
